compiled_state.title: Reviewer sub-agent grep runs in sub-agent CWD, not specified repo_root, causing false-negative pattern matches
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: When a code-reviewer sub-agent is dispatched and the diff references files in a worktree path (e.g., agent-a6ec5a0f), the reviewer runs grep from its own working directory context. If the worktree is not mounted or differs from the reviewer's CWD, grep finds no matches and the reviewer reports 'pattern does not exist in SKILL.md' even when the pattern is clearly present in the diff. This caused multiple false-positive review failures during epic 615f-fad3 (Batches 4, 5, 6), requiring 5 review rounds with explicit line-number evidence and 'DO NOT run grep' instructions to bypass the issue. Fix: reviewer sub-agents should grep against the diff content provided, not against filesystem files, OR the dispatch prompt should include the explicit REPO_ROOT with instructions to cd there before grepping.
